author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Not unto us, O Lord, not unto us, but unto Thy Name give the praise” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that grace of the water that gushed from the rock (“now that rock was Christ”), was not given on the score of works that had gone before, but of His mercy “that justifieth the ungodly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “Christ died for sinners,” that men might not seek any glory of their own, but in the Lord’s Name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy loving mercy, and for Thy truth’s sake” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe how often these two qualities, loving mercy and truth, are joined together in the holy Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in His loving mercy He called sinners, and in His truth He judgeth those who when called refused to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That the heathen may not say, Where is now their God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For at the last, His loving mercy and truth will shine forth, when “the sign of the Son of man shall appear in heaven, and then shall all tribes of the earth cry woe;” nor shall they then say, “Where is their God?” when He is no longer preached unto them to be believed in, but displayed before them to be trembled at.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As for our God, He is in heaven above” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in heaven, where they see the sun and moon, works of God which they adore, but “in heaven above,” which overpasseth all heavenly and earthly bodies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor is our God in heaven in such a sense, as to dread a fall that should deprive Him of His throne, if heaven were withdrawn from under Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In heaven and earth He hath made whatsoever pleased Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor doth He stand in need of His own works, as if He had place in them where He might abide; but endureth in His own eternity, wherein He abideth and hath done whatsoever pleased Him, both in heaven and earth; for they did not support Him, as a condition of their being created by Him: since, unless they had been created, they could not have supported Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, in whatsoever He Himself dwelleth, He, so to speak, containeth this as in need of Himself, He is not contained by this as if He needed it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or it may be thus understood: “In heaven and in earth He hath done whatsoever pleased Him,” whether among the higher or the lower orders of His people, He hath made His grace His free gift, that no man may boast in the merits of his own works.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their idols,” he saith, “are silver and gold, even the work of men’s hands” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, although we cannot display our God to your carnal eyes, whom ye ought to recognise through his works; yet be not seduced by your vain pretences, because ye can point with the finger to, the objects of your worship.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it were much worthier for you not to have what to point to, than that your hearts’ blindness should be displayed in what is exhibited to these eyes by you: for what do ye exhibit, save gold and silver?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have indeed both bronze, and wood, and earthenware idols, and of different materials of this description; but the Holy Spirit preferred mentioning the more precious material, because when every man hath blushed for that which he sets more by, he is much more easily turned away from the worship of meaner objects.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said in another passage of Scripture concerning the worshippers of images, “Saying to a stock, Thou art my father; and to a stone, Thou hast brought me forth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lest that man who speaketh thus not to a stone or stock, but to gold and silver, seem wiser to himself; let him look this way, let him turn hitherwards the ear of his heart: “The idols of the Gentiles are gold and silver.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing mean and contemptible is here mentioned: and indeed to that mind which is not earth, both gold and silver is earth, but more beautiful and brilliant, more solid and firm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Employ not then the hands of men, to create a false Deity out of that metal which a true God hath created; nay, a false man, whom thou mayest worship for a true God.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For they have mouths, and speak not: eyes have they, and see not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have ears, and hear not: noses have they, and smell not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have hands, and handle not; feet have they, and walk not; neither cry they through their throat” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even their artist therefore surpasseth them, since he had the faculty of moulding them by the motion and functions of his limbs: though thou wouldest be ashamed to worship that artist.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even thou surpassest them, though thou hast not made these things, since thou doest what they cannot do.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even a beast doth excel them; for unto this it is added, “neither cry they through their throat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For after he had said above, “they have mouths, and speak not;” what need was there, after he had enumerated the limbs from head to feet, to repeat what he had said of their crying through their throat; unless, I suppose, because we perceive that what he mentioned of the other members, was common to men and beasts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they see, and hear, and smell, and walk, and some, apes for instance, handle with hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what he had said of the mouth, is peculiar to men: since beasts do not speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that no one might refer what hath been said to the works of human members alone, and prefer men only to the gods of the heathen; after all this he added these words, “neither cry they through their throat:” which again is common to men and cattle.…How much better then do mice and serpents, and other animals of like sort, judge of the idols of the heathen, so to speak, for they regard not the human figure in them when they see not the human life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason they usually build nests in them, and unless they are deterred by human movements, they seek for themselves no safer habitations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man then moveth himself, that he may frighten away a living beast from his own god; and yet worshippeth that god who cannot move himself, as if he were powerful, from whom he drove away one better than the object of his worship.…Even the dead surpasseth a deity who neither liveth nor hath lived.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they seem to themselves to have a purer religion, who say, I neither worship an idol, nor a devil; but in the bodily image I behold an emblem of that which I am bound to worship.…They presume to reply, that they worship not the bodies themselves, but the deities which preside over the government of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One sentence of the Apostle, therefore, testifieth to their punishment and condemnation; “Who,” he saith, “have changed the truth of God into a lie, and worshipped and served the creature more than the Creator, who is blessed for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the former part of this sentence he condemned idols; in the latter, the account they give of their idols: for by designating images wrought by an artificer by the names of the works of God’s creation, they change the truth of God into a lie; while, by considering these works themselves as deities, and worshipping them as such, they serve the creature more than the Creator, who is blessed for ever.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, it will be said, we also have very many instruments and vessels made of materials or metal of this description for the purpose of celebrating the Sacraments, which being consecrated by these ministrations are called holy, in honour of Him who is thus worshipped for our salvation: and what indeed are these very instruments or vessels, but the work of men’s hands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But have they mouth, and yet speak not? have they eyes, and see not? do we pray unto them, because through them we pray to God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the chief cause of this insane profanity, that the figure resembling the living person, which induces men to worship it, hath more influence in the minds of these miserable persons, than the evident fact that it is not living, so that it ought to be despised by the living.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The result that ensueth is that described in the next verse: “They that make them are like unto them, and so are all such as put their trust in them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them therefore see with open eyes, and worship with shut and dead understandings, idols that neither see nor live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But the house of Israel hath hoped in the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For hope that is seen is not hope; for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that this patience may endure to the end, “He is their helper and defender.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do perhaps spiritual persons (by whom carnal minds are built up in “the spirit of meekness,” because they pray as higher for lower minds) already see, and is that already to them reality which to the lower is hope?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not so.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even “the house of Aaron hath hope in the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, that they also may stretch forward perseveringly towards those things which are before them, and may run perseveringly, until they may apprehend that for which they are apprehended, and may know even as they are known, “He is their helper and defender.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For both “fear the Lord, and have hoped in the Lord: He is their helper and defender” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we do not by our deservings prevent the mercy of God; but, “The Lord hath been mindful of us, and hath blessed us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath blessed the house of Israel, He hath blessed the house of Aaron” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in blessing both of these, “He hath blessed all that fear the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou ask, who are meant by both of these?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He answereth, “both small and great.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, the house of Israel with the house of Aaron, those who among that nation believed in Jesus the Saviour.…For in the character of those who out of that nation believed, it is said, “Except the Lord of Sabaoth had left us a seed, we had been as Sodoma, and been made like unto Gomorrha.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seed, because when it has been scattered over the earth, it multiplied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the great ones, of the house of Aaron, have said, “May the Lord increase you more and more, you and your children” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thus it hath happened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For children that have been raised even from the stones have flocked unto Abraham: sheep which were not of this fold, have flocked unto him, that there might be one flock, and one shepherd; the faith of all nations was added, and the number grew, not only of wise priests, but of obedient peoples; the Lord increasing not only their fathers more and more, who in Christ might show the way to the rest who should imitate them, but also their children, who should follow their fathers’ pious footsteps.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the Prophet saith unto these great and small, the mountains and the little hills, the rams and the young sheep, what followeth: “Ye are the blessed of the Lord, who made heaven and earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if he should say, Ye are the blessed of the Lord, who made the heaven in the great, earth in the small: not this visible heaven, studded with luminaries which are objects to these eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “The heaven of heavens is the Lord’s” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16); who hath elevated the minds of some saints to such a height, that they became teachable by no man, but by God Himself; in comparison of which heaven, whatever is discerned with carnal eyes is to be called earth; which “He hath given to the children of men;” that when it is contemplated, whether in that region which illumineth above, as that which is called heaven, or in that which is illumined beneath, which is properly called earth (since in comparison with that which is called heaven of heaven, the whole, as we have said, is earth;) the whole therefore of this earth He hath given to the children of men, that by the consideration of it, as far as they can, they may conceive of the Creator, whom with their yet weak hearts they cannot see without that aid to their conception.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12. …But nevertheless since they derive the truth and richness of wisdom, not from man nor through man, but through God Himself, they have received little ones who shall be heaven, that they may know that they are heaven of heaven; as yet however earth, unto which they say, “I have planted, Apollos watered, but God gave the increase.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to those very sons of men whom He made heaven, He who knoweth how to provide for the earth through heaven, hath given earth upon which they work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May they therefore abide, heaven and earth, in their God, who made them, and let them live from Him, confessing unto Him, and praising Him; for if they choose to live from themselves, they shall die, as it is written, “From the dead, as though he were not, confession ceaseth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, “The dead praise not Thee, O Lord, neither all they that go down into silence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Scripture in another passage proclaimeth, “The sinner, when he cometh into the abyss of wickednesses, scorneth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But we, who live, will praise the Lord, from this time forth for evermore” (ver.
